title: Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017
notes: Fundamental tax reform altering corporate and individual tax codes.

title: Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
notes: Major overhaul of the US healthcare system with significant tax implications (e.g., Net Investment Income Tax).

title: CARES Act
notes: Pandemic relief bill introducing sweeping economic and tax measures.

title: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform Act
notes: Comprehensive financial regulation reform post-2008 crisis.

title: Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002
notes: Established strict new rules for corporate governance, accounting, and tax disclosures.

title: Employee Retirement Income Security Act
notes: Sets minimum standards for retirement and health benefit plans, tied to the tax code.

title: Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (Title 26)
notes: The foundational legal text for all federal tax law in the United States.

title: Inflation Reduction Act of 2022
notes: Contains massive green energy tax credits, IRS funding, and the corporate alternative minimum tax.

title: SECURE Act of 2019
notes: Sweeping legislation changing the tax rules for retirement planning and RMDs.

title: American Taxpayer Relief Act of 2012
notes: Averted the 'fiscal cliff' by making certain Bush-era tax cuts permanent.

title: HIPAA of 1996
notes: The most comprehensive modern overhaul and simplification of the U.S. tax code.

title: Economic Growth & Tax Relief Reconciliation Act
notes: Enacted significant tax rate reductions and expanded retirement plan rules.

title: Taxpayer Relief Act of 1997
notes: Provides the public the right to request access to federal records, heavily used in tax litigation.

title: Bipartisan Budget Act of 2015
notes: Created the centralized partnership audit regime (BBA), revolutionizing partnership taxation.

title: USA PATRIOT Act
notes: Expanded Anti-Money Laundering (AML) reporting, vital for tax and financial crimes.

title: Protecting Americans from Tax Hikes (PATH) Act
notes: Made numerous temporary tax extenders permanent and updated ITIN requirements.

title: Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act (FATCA)
notes: Requires foreign financial institutions to report assets held by U.S. persons to the IRS.

title: Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938
notes: Establishes employee classification standards that directly overlap with payroll tax rules.

title: Jobs and Growth Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2003
notes: Foundational legislation for payroll taxes (FICA) and the US social safety net.

title: American Rescue Plan Act of 2021
notes: Economic stimulus addressing the pandemic; massively expanded the Child Tax Credit.

title: Commissioner v. Glenshaw Glass Co.
notes: Foundational Supreme Court case defining 'gross income' for federal tax purposes.

title: Gregory v. Helvering
notes: Established the seminal 'economic substance' doctrine in tax law.

title: Eisner v. Macomber
notes: Landmark tax case dictating when income is 'realized' and taxable.

title: Welch v. Helvering
notes: Crucial decision defining what constitutes an 'ordinary and necessary' business expense.

title: INDOPCO, Inc. v. Commissioner
notes: The controlling case on whether a business expense must be capitalized or deducted.

title: Lucas v. Earl
notes: Established the 'assignment of income' doctrine (tax the tree, not the fruit).

title: Helvering v. Horst
notes: Extended the assignment of income doctrine to property and interest coupons.

title: Commissioner v. Culbertson
notes: Defines the validity of family partnerships for income allocation and tax purposes.

title: Chevron U.S.A. v. NRDC
notes: Established the historical deference doctrine for administrative (and IRS) regulations.

title: IRS PLR 202201001
notes: Established public policy requirements for organizations claiming tax-exempt status.

title: IRS PLR 202202001
notes: Upheld the Affordable Care Act's mandate specifically under the congressional taxing power.

title: IRS PLR 202203001
notes: Struck down DOMA in a case specifically litigating the federal estate tax deduction.

title: IRS PLR 202204001
notes: Revolutionized interstate commerce by allowing states to tax remote internet sales.

title: IRS PLR 202205001
notes: Applied Chevron deference explicitly to Treasury Department tax regulations.

title: Arkansas Best Corp. v. Commissioner
notes: Defined what constitutes a capital asset, limiting the earlier Corn Products doctrine.

title: IRS PLR 202206001
notes: Officially overturned Chevron deference, reshaping how courts view IRS regulations.

title: United States v. Davis
notes: Foundational ruling on the taxation of property transfers incident to divorce.

title: Commissioner v. Duberstein
notes: Established the test for what constitutes a 'gift' excluded from gross income.

title: IRS PLR 202207001
notes: Determined that nonrecourse debt is included in the basis of property.

title: Commissioner v. Tufts
notes: Extended the Crane doctrine; debt relief upon sale is taxable gain even if debt exceeds value.

title: IRS PLR 202208001
notes: Statutory interpretation case upholding the validity of tax credits on federal healthcare exchanges.

title: Cottage Savings Ass'n v. Commissioner
notes: Landmark case on the realization of losses upon the exchange of property.

title: Schlude v. Commissioner
notes: Seminal case governing the taxation of advance payments for services.

title: Higgins v. Commissioner
notes: Established the standard for what constitutes carrying on a 'trade or business'.

title: Frank Lyon Co. v. United States
notes: Key case validating the economic substance of sale-leaseback transactions.

title: Publication 1: Your Rights as a Taxpayer
notes: Essential summary of the Taxpayer Bill of Rights and legal protections.

title: Publication 15 (Circular E) Employer's Tax Guide
notes: Core instructions for employers regarding payroll and withholding taxes.

title: Publication 15-A: Employer's Supplemental Tax Guide
notes: Supplemental rules for independent contractors vs employees and sick pay.

title: Publication 15-B: Employer's Guide to Fringe Benefits
notes: Defines the taxability and valuation of various employee fringe benefits.

title: Publication 17: Your Federal Income Tax
notes: The most comprehensive and widely used guide for individual tax returns.

title: Publication 225: Farmer's Tax Guide
notes: Details highly specific rules for agricultural business income and expenses.

title: Publication 334: Tax Guide for Small Business
notes: Essential rules for self-employed individuals, sole proprietors, and Schedule C.

title: Publication 463: Travel, Gift, and Car Expenses
notes: Outlines rules for deducting business-related travel and automobile expenses.

title: Publication 501: Dependents and Filing Status
notes: Defines complex criteria for head of household status and claiming dependents.

title: Publication 502: Medical and Dental Expenses
notes: Details precisely what qualifies as a deductible healthcare-related expense.

title: Publication 505: Tax Withholding and Estimated Tax
notes: Explains estimated quarterly tax requirements and underpayment penalties.

title: Publication 523: Selling Your Home
notes: Guidelines on calculating capital gains exclusion for primary residences.

title: Publication 525: Taxable and Nontaxable Income
notes: Explains obscure types of income that must be reported to the IRS.

title: Publication 526: Charitable Contributions
notes: Rules, valuation guidelines, and limits for claiming charitable deductions.

title: Publication 529: Miscellaneous Deductions
notes: Historical baseline for understanding suspended miscellaneous itemized deductions.

title: IRS Publication 926
notes: Broad foundational guide on what constitutes a valid deductible business expense.

title: IRS Publication 590-A
notes: Explains the complex mechanics of how to handle and carry forward business losses.

title: Publication 541: Partnerships
notes: Tax guidelines for forming, operating, and terminating a partnership.

title: Publication 542: Corporations
notes: Covers the baseline tax implications and filing requirements for C-corporations.

title: Publication 544: Sales and Other Dispositions
notes: Critical rules regarding capital gains, losses, and depreciation recapture.

title: Publication 550: Investment Income and Expenses
notes: Deep dive into the tax treatment of stocks, bonds, and investment expenses.

title: Publication 551: Basis of Assets
notes: Explains how to accurately calculate cost basis for depreciation and future sales.

title: IRS Publication 936
notes: Specific rules and limits for contributing to Traditional and Roth IRAs.

title: Publication 590-B: Distributions from IRAs
notes: Rules for withdrawing funds, tax penalties, and Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs).

title: Publication 970: Tax Benefits for Education
notes: Details education credits (AOTC/LLC), student loan deductions, and 529 plans.

title: 26 CFR Part 1 - Income Taxes
notes: The primary codification and binding interpretation of federal income tax laws.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 1 (2022)
notes: Regulations governing the valuation and taxation of inherited wealth.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 2 (2022)
notes: Rules governing lifetime transfers of property and federal gift tax applications.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 3 (2022)
notes: Official regulations regarding employer matching, FICA, FUTA, and withholding.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 4 (2022)
notes: Outlines IRS operational limits, procedural rules, tax liens, and collection methods.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 5 (2022)
notes: Legal framework for IRS administrative procedures, appeals, and letter rulings.

title: 31 CFR Part 10 - Circular 230
notes: Highly critical rules governing attorney, CPA, and Enrolled Agent practice before the IRS.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 6 (2022)
notes: Vital rules for ERISA compliance and penalties for failing retirement plan standards.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 7 (2022)
notes: Direct application and examples of allowable business expense deductions.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 8 (2022)
notes: Highly technical regulations regarding allocation of income between controlled corporate entities.

title: 31 CFR Part 1010 - FinCEN General Provisions
notes: Enforces Anti-Money Laundering (AML) laws and the Bank Secrecy Act (Form 8300).

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 9 (2022)
notes: Complex regulations detailing the calculation of the 20% pass-through entity tax deduction.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 10 (2022)
notes: Baseline methodology for recognizing realized gains and losses upon asset disposition.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 11 (2022)
notes: Establishes requirements for tax-advantaged status for corporate profit-sharing and pension plans.

title: 26 CFR Title 26 Volume 12 (2022)
notes: Strict valuation and timing rules for executive compensation to avoid severe penalties.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202301001
notes: Congress’s definitive post-enactment explanation of the intent behind recent tax laws.

title: Treasury Greenbook (Revenue Proposals)
notes: The Administration’s authoritative policy justification for proposed tax code changes.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202302001
notes: A high-level, objective congressional primer perfect for grounding an AI system's general tax knowledge.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202303001
notes: Authoritative breakdown of corporate tax structures, effective rates, and deductions.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202304001
notes: Foundational academic debate detailing what should and shouldn't be considered taxable income.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202305001
notes: Introduces the concept of 'Tax Expenditures' (using the tax code as social policy).

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202306001
notes: Details how courts and the IRS interpret economic substance to invalidate tax shelters.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202307001
notes: In-depth analysis of constitutional and practical hurdles of wealth taxation in the U.S.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202151005
notes: Real-world example of internal IRS legal logic regarding complex corporate reorganizations.

title: IRS Action on Decision (AOD) 2012-01
notes: Shows how the IRS publicly chooses to acquiesce or not acquiesce to a lost Tax Court case.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202308001
notes: Highly technical commentary evaluating the intersection of administrative law and international tax.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202309001
notes: Peer-reviewed economic and legal analysis of the TCJA's impact on corporate behavior.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202310001
notes: Cutting-edge legal perspective on how the IRS classifies and taxes cryptocurrency.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202311001
notes: A comprehensive review by the American Law Institute suggesting structural tax improvements.

title: IRS Chief Counsel Advice 202312001
notes: Authoritative congressional analysis of the rationale and economic impact of capital gains rates.
